title: Treehouse Of Horror Xiv

jokes.text: Good grief, This candy's terrible. Circus peanuts, raisins, nicotine gum, a library card...
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about bad Halloween candy
jokes.punchline: The escalation from circus peanuts to a library card as 'candy'
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons escalation from mundane bad candy to absurd 'library card as candy.' Good character voice.

jokes.text: You got all the good stuff! - And yet and I'm still not satisfied.
jokes.setup: Lisa envying Bart's terrible candy haul
jokes.punchline: Bart's existential dissatisfaction despite having 'good stuff'
jokes.explanation: Mild irony that plays on Bart's character. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You thieving hussy!
jokes.setup: Someone accusing another of candy theft
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top Victorian insult 'thieving hussy' for candy theft
jokes.explanation: Mild escalation joke with archaic language. Gets a chuckle but not memorable.

jokes.text: Eat fire! Punks! What the... I'm still cold.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically breathing fire at kids
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic 'I'm still cold' after the dramatic fire display
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - dramatic escalation followed by mundane complaint. Classic character comedy.

jokes.text: Pathetic humans! They are showing a halloween episode... in November!
jokes.setup: Alien commenting on human behavior
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall about the episode airing late
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta joke about the show's actual late air date. Self-aware and clever.

jokes.text: Oh, still thinking about halloween. We've already got our christmas decorations up.
jokes.setup: Following up on the Halloween timing complaint
jokes.punchline: The aliens have moved even further ahead to Christmas
jokes.explanation: Decent follow-up to the meta joke but less impactful than the setup.

jokes.text: I AM DEATH. - Death? We don't want any.
jokes.setup: Death arriving to claim someone
jokes.punchline: Homer treating Death like a door-to-door salesman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - treating the supernatural as mundane. Excellent misdirection and character work.

jokes.text: Bart! Run like a wind(pronounced as waind)! - Mom. It's wind.
jokes.setup: Marge telling Bart to run from Death
jokes.punchline: Marge mispronouncing 'wind' and Bart correcting her
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge malapropism with Bart's matter-of-fact correction. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Well, I only read it in books.
jokes.setup: Marge being corrected on pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Her explaining she only knows it from reading, not hearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge logic - earnest and endearing. Good character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Please don't take me! Take Milhouse! We all know there's no happy ending there.
jokes.setup: Bart begging Death not to take him
jokes.punchline: Offering up Milhouse instead with brutal honesty about his prospects
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cruel Bart moment - sacrificing his best friend with casual brutality. Dark and funny.

jokes.text: Does this mean I'll never cancel the Jim Belushi show? - I guess so. - NOOOOOOOOOOOO!
jokes.setup: Lisa realizing the implications of no death
jokes.punchline: Homer's horror at the Jim Belushi show never ending
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference with perfect escalation to Homer's despair. Sharp writing.

jokes.text: If I know I'll tell you, I swear! I'd better call my wife, tell her I'll be late.
jokes.setup: Gangster being tortured but can't die
jokes.punchline: Casually calling his wife like it's just another work delay
jokes.explanation: Good mundane/extreme contrast. The domestic normalcy in torture scene works well.

jokes.text: If I knew this would take so long, I'd put on a TV.
jokes.setup: Torture session dragging on
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony treating it like any long wait, wanting entertainment
jokes.explanation: Decent follow-up to the torture comedy. Makes the violence mundane and boring.

jokes.text: Pizza! There's money on the counter. No tip. - Are you miserable bastard? - That's why I'm up there.
jokes.setup: Pizza delivery to torture scene, no tip offered
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony's matter-of-fact explanation of his criminal status
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Fat Tony. Perfect criminal logic delivered deadpan.

jokes.text: You were a busboy in the restaurant of life. Clearing away the oldies and the sickies and the chokies.
jokes.setup: Homer eulogizing Death with a metaphor
jokes.punchline: The increasingly casual terms 'oldies, sickies, and chokies'
jokes.explanation: Good Homer attempt at profundity that devolves into casual terminology. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: And you made NASCAR racing exciting.
jokes.setup: Continuing to praise Death's work
jokes.punchline: Implying NASCAR is only exciting because of potential death
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about NASCAR's appeal. Dark but accurate cultural commentary.

jokes.text: Check it out! I finally found a dead guy's clothes that fits me!
jokes.setup: Homer trying on Death's robe
jokes.punchline: His excitement about finding clothes from a corpse that fit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - focusing on the fit rather than the morbid source. Dark and funny.

jokes.text: I might occasionally kill out of anger, or to illustrate a point. But I am not a Grim Reaper...
jokes.setup: Homer protesting he won't be the Grim Reaper
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting he might kill for petty reasons while denying the job
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - casual murder is fine, but professional death work crosses the line. Perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Where's doug? Never mind. I'm death now. I liked doug.
jokes.setup: Jasper asking about the regular Grim Reaper
jokes.punchline: His quick acceptance and nostalgic fondness for the previous Death
jokes.explanation: Good old man acceptance. Jasper treating death like a routine service change.

jokes.text: My dad's job takes into all parts of the community. He performs a valuable service. But it's often misunderstood. Like a vulture or the flesh eating maggot.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending her father's job in career day presentation
jokes.punchline: Comparing Homer to increasingly unpleasant scavengers
jokes.explanation: Great Lisa earnestness leading to unflattering but accurate comparisons. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Pardon me. Coming through. Rest in peace. You're dead. Take a dirt nap. Meet the worms.
jokes.setup: Homer clearing a path at the baseball game
jokes.punchline: Using increasingly creative euphemisms for death instead of 'excuse me'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of death euphemisms. Homer taking his job too literally in casual situations.

jokes.text: Ok. Who am I giving the finger to today?
jokes.setup: Homer consulting his Death list
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding 'finger of death' as literally giving someone the finger
jokes.explanation: Good Homer literal interpretation. Character-appropriate misunderstanding of his supernatural duties.

jokes.text: This isn't Marge. This is her fat sister. Selma. - It's Patty. Chump!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to kill who he thinks is Marge
jokes.punchline: Patty correcting him while being strangled - maintaining the insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Patty's priorities are maintaining her identity even while being murdered.

jokes.text: The Nobel Prize? Finally! So it's for what? My whole deal?
jokes.setup: Frink getting the Nobel Prize call
jokes.punchline: His vague assumption it's for his 'whole deal' rather than specific work
jokes.explanation: Good Frink character moment. The casual 'whole deal' fits his scattered scientist persona.

jokes.text: If I say no, do I still win?
jokes.setup: Nobel committee asking if this is Professor Frink
jokes.punchline: Frink trying to game the system by denying his identity
jokes.explanation: Good Frink logic - trying to keep the prize while avoiding the responsibility.

jokes.text: They must've awarded me for my invention of a hammer with a screw driver on the other end. It's convenient.
jokes.setup: Frink speculating about why he won Nobel Prize
jokes.punchline: The incredibly mundane invention that somehow won Nobel Prize
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Nobel Prize prestige. Frink's practical but unimpressive invention.

jokes.text: It was a slow year. Yes.
jokes.setup: Frink asking if hammer-screwdriver won Nobel
jokes.punchline: Committee's matter-of-fact admission it was a weak year
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan admission. Works as institutional comedy about Nobel Prize standards.

jokes.text: Mother used to say we get along like positrons and anti-nutrinos. Yeah, I'm geek.
jokes.setup: Frink explaining his relationship with his father
jokes.punchline: Using incredibly nerdy physics metaphor then acknowledging his geekiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frink character work. The physics metaphor followed by self-awareness is spot-on.

jokes.text: he was one of those Heman scientists who work on the atom bomb by day and slept with Marilyn Monroe by night and sold secrets to the Russians at lunch.
jokes.setup: Frink describing his father as a different type of scientist
jokes.punchline: The escalating schedule of stereotypical mad scientist activities
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and absurdist humor. The time structure (day/night/lunch) is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: I get seesick taking a shower! Clean, but nauseous!
jokes.setup: Young Frink explaining why he can't go on ocean expedition
jokes.punchline: Getting seasick from shower water, with the contradictory result
jokes.explanation: Good Frink weakness escalated to absurd degree. The 'clean but nauseous' summary is perfect.

jokes.text: You've disgraced the name of John Nerdelbaum Frink.
jokes.setup: Frink Sr. disowning his son
jokes.punchline: Revealing the ridiculous full name 'John Nerdelbaum Frink'
jokes.explanation: 'Nerdelbaum' is brilliant character naming. Makes the shame even more ridiculous.

jokes.text: Does shark do that? Yes. He was testing out a new blood-based suntan lotion.
jokes.setup: Seeing Frink Sr. eaten by shark
jokes.punchline: The absurd invention that led to his death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd invention. Blood-based suntan lotion is perfectly stupid scientist logic.

jokes.text: You know now that I have my hammer-screw-driver, I could reanimate him, without the needless switching of tools.
jokes.setup: Frink deciding to resurrect his father
jokes.punchline: Bringing back the mundane Nobel Prize invention as the solution
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier joke. Frink's practical approach to mad science resurrection.

jokes.text: Well, that's always tedious. Annoying!
jokes.setup: Discussion of tool switching during resurrection
jokes.punchline: Committee member treating resurrection work like mundane labor complaint
jokes.explanation: Mild institutional comedy. Treating supernatural work as office efficiency issue.

jokes.text: Now, we had to replace several vital organs with machinery, but that doesn't make you any less of a man. - Except you have no penis.
jokes.setup: Frink reassuring his resurrected father about his humanity
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining it with the most essential missing part
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and immediate deflation. The casual delivery of devastating news is excellent.

jokes.text: It's a lot like the 20th, except everybody's afraid and the stock market is much lower.
jokes.setup: Explaining the 21st century to Frink Sr.
jokes.punchline: Brutally accurate summary of post-2000 America
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural observation. Works as both comedy and social commentary.

jokes.text: Well I did finish first in the walk for the cure, of homosexuality!
jokes.setup: Ned claiming to be healthy specimen
jokes.punchline: Revealing his participation in absurd/offensive 'cure homosexuality' walk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders religious extremism delivered with his usual cheerful obliviousness.

jokes.text: Confucious? And Milton Berle? Boy I have been barking up the wrong tree!
jokes.setup: Ned dying and seeing heaven
jokes.punchline: Being surprised by the diverse afterlife population
jokes.explanation: Good Ned moment realizing his narrow religious views were wrong. The pairing is absurdist and funny.

jokes.text: Mother always said: 'A curvy spine is the double roller coaster.'
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy being complimented on posture
jokes.punchline: His mother's bizarre metaphor for good posture
jokes.explanation: Good Comic Book Guy family weirdness. The metaphor is appropriately nonsensical.

jokes.text: Comic book guy's ass won't fit in couch. - You may keep my posterior, just please return the Jaba the Butt tattoo.
jokes.setup: Discussing seating arrangements with stolen body parts
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's Star Wars pun tattoo on his rear
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy reference. 'Jaba the Butt' is exactly his kind of nerdy wordplay.

jokes.text: You know Dr. Herschbach, our jobs are actually not that different. - I disagree.
jokes.setup: Jennifer Garner comparing her acting to Nobel Prize science
jokes.punchline: Scientist's immediate, flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity ego deflation. The scientist's immediate dismissal is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I'm here to accept the Nobel Prize for stupid.
jokes.setup: Frink Sr. arriving at ceremony
jokes.punchline: Claiming Nobel Prize 'for stupid' instead of physics
jokes.explanation: Good broken robot speech pattern. Makes Nobel Prize sound like stupidity award.

jokes.text: You all have such big hearts and such big brains with large juicy dripping with knowledge heads!
jokes.setup: Frink Sr. complimenting the Nobel audience
jokes.punchline: The compliment devolving into hunger for their brains
jokes.explanation: Good monster reveal through compliments. The transition from praise to hunger works well.

jokes.text: And I thought Halle Berry went nuts during her acceptance speech.
jokes.setup: Witnessing Frink Sr.'s brain-eating rampage
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to Halle Berry's emotional Oscar speech
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference that perfectly scales the absurdity. Great timing and specificity.

jokes.text: Muffins are suprisingly high in calories. The pyramids were actually built by Sears.
jokes.setup: Frink Sr. claiming each brain reveals universe secrets
jokes.punchline: The 'secrets' being mundane trivia and conspiracy nonsense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of 'cosmic secrets.' Mixing mundane facts with absurd conspiracy theories.

jokes.text: This is more violent than the hip-hop worlds.
jokes.setup: Watching the Nobel Prize brain-eating massacre
jokes.punchline: Comparing academic violence to hip-hop beef
jokes.explanation: Mild cultural reference joke. Works but feels a bit forced and dated.

jokes.text: I disagree!
jokes.setup: Someone claiming this is the most exciting Nobel ceremony ever
jokes.punchline: Dr. Herschbach's consistent disagreement throughout the episode
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective callback. His consistent disagreement becomes a character trait.

jokes.text: I maybe a soul, but I'm hungry. Can you throw in a little mazzard. Maybe a nice pizza pit(?)
jokes.setup: Frink Sr.'s soul in the catcher
jokes.punchline: Even as a disembodied soul, still focused on food
jokes.explanation: Maintains character consistency - death doesn't change his appetites. Mild humor.

jokes.text: That's a good shwandler - Oh, I waited so long to hear you say that. What dose it mean, is it dirty?
jokes.setup: Frink Sr. calling his son a 'good shwandler'
jokes.punchline: Frink's excitement despite not knowing what it means or if it's appropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect father-son reconciliation moment. Frink's desperate need for approval despite confusion.

jokes.text: Superman vs Patty Hearst. Evel Knievel jumps the Jackson 5. Batman and Rhoda.
jokes.setup: Bart looking at 1970s comic books
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd comic book crossovers from that era
jokes.explanation: Brilliant 1970s pop culture escalation. Each crossover gets more ridiculous and specific to the era.

jokes.text: Wow, she looks like a background character in a Hanna-Barbera cartoon.
jokes.setup: Bart seeing a woman frozen in time
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to cheap animation style
jokes.explanation: Good animation industry self-reference. Specific enough to be funny without being inside baseball.

jokes.text: Do you realize what this means? - Yeah, but you say it first!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if Bart understands the implications
jokes.punchline: Bart's consistent pattern of making others explain things
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic. Bart's intellectual laziness as comedy mechanism.

jokes.text: Lets get really far ahead on our homework! Wait till the other kids see we're already on the RED unit of Adventures In Reading.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting they can do anything with time stopped
jokes.punchline: Her idea of 'anything' being academic overachievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment. Having unlimited power and choosing homework is quintessentially her.

jokes.text: Due to budget cuts, music class will now have a two drink minimum.
jokes.setup: Principal Skinner making school announcements
jokes.punchline: Treating elementary school like a nightclub due to funding issues
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist school funding joke. Perfectly captures bureaucratic desperation.

jokes.text: Come, let us kill them before learning of the magical secret which they possess!
jokes.setup: Springfield mob discovering the pranksters
jokes.punchline: Mob choosing violence over potentially useful knowledge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mob mentality joke. Perfectly captures the self-defeating nature of angry crowds.

jokes.text: Yeah, but you say it first.
jokes.setup: Bart understanding they broke the watch
jokes.punchline: Exact same response pattern as before
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that establishes character consistency. Less impactful the second time.

jokes.text: I'm gonna play naked basketball. - No, you are not.
jokes.setup: Milhouse suggesting they can do anything
jokes.punchline: His immediate choice being inappropriate, Bart shutting it down
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Milhouse's repressed desires emerge immediately. Bart as voice of reason.

jokes.text: Yeah, I thought I'd be happier if my parents back together. But it kind of hollow.
jokes.setup: Milhouse reflecting on his time-stopped activities
jokes.punchline: Using magical powers to force his divorced parents back together
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment for Milhouse. Using god-like power for personal family therapy.

jokes.text: Yeah. And I can't run too fast after 15 years of eating nothing but gummy worms. I'm gonna quit tomorrow, I swear.
jokes.setup: Bart worrying about getting caught
jokes.punchline: Revealing his diet deteriorated over 15 years, with classic addiction language
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the time passage. The addiction language for gummy worms is perfect.

jokes.text: Like why is my nose jammed full of army men. Oh, come on! Don't you get it? Bart stopped time with his magic stopwatch.
jokes.setup: Homer listing life's mysteries
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning army men in his nose while Lisa tries to explain the real situation
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's random physical comedy and Lisa's logical explanation.

jokes.text: Jealous much? Why can't I tinker with the fabric of existence?
jokes.setup: Family dismissing Lisa's explanation
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectual jealousy over not getting to manipulate reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - being jealous about missing out on the scientific opportunity rather than the power.

tmdb_overview: "Reaper Madness" - Death becomes Homer and our hero must learn to reap what he sows (and pull a fast one on the almighty). "Frinkenstein" - Soon to be Nobel-prize winning Professor Frink reanimates his father for some gruesome body parts-swapping. "Stop the World, I Want to Goof Off" - A parody of Clockstoppers, starring Bart and Milhouse.
